testCase.name: Validate Currency Swap and Recalculation
testCase.objective: To verify that the user can navigate to the currency converter page and correctly swap selected currencies, ensuring that the conversion result is recalculated accurately after the swap.
testCase.precondition: User is on the Currency Converter page

step.expectedResult: User is successfully navigated to the Currency Converter page
Conversion is recalculated correctly based on swapped currencies
step.testData:
• fromCurrency: USD
  • toCurrency: GEL
  • amount: 100

testCase.name: Validate Currency Conversion
testCase.objective: To verify that the user can navigate to the currency converter page and that currency conversion is calculated correctly across different currency pairs and various input amounts.
testCase.precondition:
• User is on the main page of the application
  • Application is up and running

step.expectedResult: User is successfully navigated to the Currency Converter page
Conversion is calculated correctly for each pair
Conversion is calculated correctly for each amount

testCase.name: Validate Converter Input Boundary
testCase.objective: To verify that the currency converter correctly handles input boundary conditions by restricting or validating the allowed input length/values.
testCase.precondition: User is on the main page of the application

step.expectedResult: User is successfully navigated to the Currency Converter page

• "From" currency is set to GEL
  • "To" currency is set to EUR

• Input field enforces defined boundary limits 
  • System behaves correctly when boundary is reached (no overflow / invalid input handling works properly)

testCase.name: Validate Invalid Input in Currency Converter
testCase.objective: To verify that the currency converter correctly handles invalid input values and prevents incorrect or unsupported data from being processed.
testCase.precondition:
• User is on the main page of the application
  • Application is up and running

step.expectedResult: User is successfully navigated to the Currency Converter page

• "From" currency is set to GEL
  • "To" currency is set to EUR

• Invalid input is handled properly
  • System does not perform incorrect conversion
  • Proper validation behavior is triggered

testCase.name: Verify Non-Numeric Character Restriction in Amount Field
testCase.objective: To verify that the currency converter input field does not accept non-numeric values and enforces numeric-only input validation
testCase.precondition:
• User is on the main page of the application
  • Application is up and running

step.expectedResult: User is successfully navigated to the Currency Converter page

• Input field does not display them
  • No conversion is triggered with invalid input
  • System maintains valid state

testCase.name: [API] Validate Commercial Exchange Rates
testCase.objective: Verify that the Commercial List API returns correct exchange rate data, including status codes, currency details, and chronological accuracy.
testCase.precondition: API service is up and running.

step.expectedResult:
1. API returns a successful 200 OK response.

2. Response body is correctly mapped to the CommercialListResponse model.
3. Exchange rates list is not empty and contains valid data.
4. Official rates are correctly calculated/positioned between buy/sell rates.
5. All currency details and update timestamps are accurate.
step.testData:
{ "locale": "ka-GE" }

where CURRENCY_NAME is a  "USD" and the response is mapped to CommercialListResponse

testCase.name: [API] Validate Exchange Rate Rejection for Identical Parameters
testCase.objective: To verify that the API correctly handles and rejects requests when the source and target currencies are identical
testCase.precondition:
• API service is reachable.

  • Base and Target currency codes (Constants) are defined in the system.

  • User has necessary permissions to call the exchange rate endpoint.

step.description: Validate Exchange Rate Rejection for Identical Currencies
step.expectedResult: The API returns a 200 status, and the response body contains the expected HTML Rejection message

testCase.name: [API] Validate Successful Exchange Rate Calculation
testCase.objective: To verify that the API successfully returns accurate exchange rate data, including buy/sell rates and currency metadata, for a valid currency pair.
testCase.precondition:
• API service is operational.

  • The specified currency constants (EUR, GEL) are valid and supported by the system

step.expectedResult:
1. API returns 200 OK.

2. Incoming and Outcome currencies are correct.

3. Sell Rate is greater than Buy Rate.

4. All metadata is valid and mapped correctly.

testCase.name: [API] Validate Successful Forward Rates Retrieval
testCase.objective: To verify that the API successfully returns current forward currency rates, including correctly grouped rate details and valid update timestamps.
testCase.precondition:
• Forward Rates API service is operational and accessible.

  • System has active forward rate groups configured

step.description: Retrieve and Validate Forward Currency Rates
step.expectedResult:
1. API returns 200 OK.

2. Forward rates list is not empty.

3. Rates are correctly grouped and contain valid details.

4. The update date is current and valid.
step.testData:
{ "Action": "GET", "Resource": "ForwardRates" }
where the response is mapped to the ForwardRatesResponse model.

testCase.name: Validate Commercial List API Performance Under Load
testCase.objective: To verify that the Commercial List API performs within acceptable response time and error thresholds under load, while returning valid and complete data.
testCase.precondition:
• API endpoint is accessible:
    /api/v1/exchangeRates/commercialList?locale=ka-GE
  • Network conditions are stable

step.expectedResult:
1. http_req_failed is less than 1%.


2. http_req_duration is under 500ms.


3. No connection timeouts
step.testData:
Target: 25 VUs

Ramp-up: 30s

Duration: 1m

Ramp-down: 30s

customField.name: description
customField.value: Endpoint: /exchangeRates/commercialList

step.description: Validate Response Integrity
step.expectedResult:
1. HTTP Status is 200.


2. Body contains rates array (not empty).


3. updateDateTime field is present and valid.
step.testData: Locale: ka-GE

testCase.name: Get Exchange Rate API Performance
testCase.objective: Validate that the system can handle concurrent requests for a specific currency pai while maintaining a strict 400ms threshold for the specific get_exchange_rate_duration metric.

step.description: Execute Load Test Scenario
step.expectedResult:
1. http_req_failed < 1%.


2. get_exchange_rate_duration (P95) < 400ms.


3. Max response time < 1000ms.
step.testData:
VUs: 25


Ramp-up: 30s


Duration: 1m


Ramp-down: 30

step.description: Verify Response for Specific Pair
step.expectedResult:
1. HTTP Status is 200.

2. Response iso1 is "USD" and iso2 is "GEL".

3. updateDate field is present in the response body.

4. No "Request failed" warnings in the console logs.
step.testData:
Iso1=USD

Iso2=GEL

Timeout: 3s

testCase.name: Exchange Rate API Stress Test & WAF Resilience
testCase.objective: Determine the API's breaking point and evaluate how the network infrastructure (Firewall/WAF) handles high-concurrency spikes (up to 150 VUs).

step.description: Execute Multi-Stage Stress Test
step.expectedResult:
1. http_req_failed < 20%.

2. firewall_block_rate < 50%.

3. No system crashes (500 errors) before reaching 75 VUs.
step.testData:
Max VUs: 150

Total Duration: ~8m

Timeout: 5s

step.description: Verify Retry & Firewall Logic
step.expectedResult:
1. Script logs warnings when blocked.

2. Retries are triggered for 5xx errors.
step.testData:
Retries: Max 2

Block Indicators: 403, 429, 503

step.expectedResult:
1. 100% of successful responses (200 OK) contain the correct USD/GEL pair.

2. Business Rule: sellRate is consistently >= buyRate

testCase.name: Forward Rates API Performance
testCase.objective: To verify that the Forward Rates API can maintain high availability and sub-500ms response times under a concurrent load of 25 users

step.description: Execute API Load Test
step.expectedResult:
1. http_req_failed is less than 1%.

2. forward_rates_duration (P95) is under 500ms.
step.testData:
VUs: 25

Duration: 1.5 min

Timeout: 2s

customField.name: description
customField.value: /forwardRates/getForwardRates

step.description: Validate Forward Rates Data
step.expectedResult:
1. HTTP Status is 200.

2. Response body contains a non-empty rates list and a valid updateDate.
